content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 14, 2001

Evelyn Adams
198 Racquet Club Drive
Compton, CA 90220

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-01-013

Dear Ms. Adams:

The Fair Political Practices Commission sent a letter to you last month seeking responses on questions relating to your January 5, 2001, request for written advice. As of today's date, we have not received a reply to our inquiry.

As indicated in our February 8, 2001 correspondence, absent a reply to our communiqué, we are withdrawing your request for written advice.

Please contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660 should you have any additional questions or comments.

Very truly yours,

Joan Giannetta
Secretary, Legal Division

/jg

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 8, 2001

Evelyn Adams
198 Racquet Club Drive
Compton, CA 90220

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-01-013

Dear Ms. Adams:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Unfortunately, we are unable to provide the requested advice without further information regarding your relationship to the candidate for whom you wish to raise funds, and the nature and scope of activities you propose to undertake on his or her behalf. Different provisions of the Political Reform Act apply in different situations, and specific facts are needed to properly analyze your questions. Please contact this office, either by telephone or by letter, within 30 days of the date of this letter, with the specific facts of your situation. Please include your telephone number in any future written correspondence, so that we may contact you if we have any further questions. If we do not hear from you within 30 days, we will consider your request for advice withdrawn.

I have enclosed Information Manual C, which addresses Campaign Provisions of the Political Reform Act for: General Purpose Recipient Committees, (including Sponsored Committees, Political Party Committees, and County Central Committees) and the 2000 Addendum to this manual. This manual may apply to your situation and may contain the answers to your questions. In addition, our website, which is located at http://www.fppc.ca.gov, is a good resource for general information. You may find the information you seek listed there.

_______________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. I-01-013
Page No. 2

If you would like to provide further specific information, or if you have any other
questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660, or write to the
address above.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel

By: Holly B. Armstrong
Legal Division
